title: Motion Opportunities
subtitle: A focused motion-design radar for roles, studios, and briefs that fit your taste for sharp systems, playful interaction, product storytelling, and polished moving images.

candidate.name: Pedro Sousa
candidate.headline: Motion Designer & Creative Systems Thinker
candidate.experience: Motion design, visual systems, product storytelling, interactive prototypes, and brand-led digital work.

source: Motion design studio targets, product teams, creative technology leads

jobs.title: Senior Motion Designer
jobs.why: This is the cleanest fit: high-craft motion for product moments, launches, explainers, and interface storytelling. It lines up with your eye for systems, timing, clarity, and work that feels designed rather than merely decorated.

jobs.title: Motion Designer - Brand Systems
jobs.why: A strong direction if you want motion to sit inside identity systems: kinetic type, launch films, social packages, and design rules that can scale. Your taste for precise visual language makes this worth prioritising.

jobs.title: Creative Technologist - Motion Prototypes
jobs.why: This is a compelling lane when motion, interaction, and prototypes overlap. It gives you space to build tactile interface ideas, AI-assisted experiments, and expressive product demos without being boxed into pure video output.

jobs.title: Motion Designer - Social & Launch Content
jobs.why: Good fit for polished campaign assets, product reveals, and short-form storytelling. It may move faster and more content-heavy than studio work, but the product-design sensibility is close to your requested direction.

jobs.title: Design Animator - UI Motion
jobs.why: Useful if you want motion to sharpen product comprehension: onboarding, microinteractions, feature demos, and UI narratives. It is a little more product-functional than expressive, but it can build a strong portfolio bridge.

jobs.title: Motion Graphics Designer - Editorial Video
jobs.why: A practical match for title sequences, explainers, lower thirds, templates, and fast visual storytelling. It is less brand-system led, but it keeps your craft visible and improves your reel quickly.

jobs.title: 3D Motion Designer
jobs.why: Interesting stretch if you want to lean harder into product objects, spatial scenes, and tactile renders. Treat this as a maybe unless the role values design judgement as much as deep 3D production.

jobs.title: Freelance Motion Design Briefs
jobs.why: Good as a parallel track for brand films, launch kits, and product explainers while you refine the main portfolio. Keep it selective so the work compounds toward the motion-design identity you actually want.
